user_outcome: An authenticated SwornAgent operator can safely pause, resume, retry, defer, or acknowledge an allowed loop operation from the mobile board and receive one revision-checked audited result even across retries or concurrent processes.
rationale: This completes the controlled side of the mobile operations outcome in #109 while preserving the fail-closed findings of #108. It is the user-facing reachability path for the pause capability tracked as dark code in #68 and applies the cross-process safety lessons of #5. Every gesture becomes C-02, every accepted or authenticated-rejected decision becomes C-03, every state precondition uses C-04, and every HTTP edge follows C-06 plus S09-owned C-08. This slice owns the C-12 session cookie, C-13 CSRF header, C-14 If-Match header, and C-15 Idempotency-Key contracts. The browser and handler never write status files or spawn loop commands directly.
in_scope: Keep the default `sworn serve` bind on loopback. Require an explicit TLS certificate/key, secure authentication secret reference, and exact allowed HTTPS origin before accepting a non-loopback bind; reject wildcard origins and secret values supplied on the command line.

Add a bounded bootstrap-to-session flow using a high-entropy operator secret stored in the secure credential envelope or mode-0600 file; issue short-lived HttpOnly SameSite=Strict sessions, use Secure cookies for non-loopback TLS, rotate session/CSRF material, and never persist bearer material in browser storage.

Require authentication for every state-changing endpoint, including loopback; require exact Origin validation, a session-bound X-CSRF-Token, Idempotency-Key, Content-Type application/json, bounded body, and If-Match C-04 revision before C-02 enqueue.

Enumerate the browser wire edge: exact allowed origin response, credentials mode, OPTIONS handling, Access-Control-Allow-Headers for Content-Type/If-Match/Idempotency-Key/X-CSRF-Token, no wildcard with credentials, and no proxy rewrite or cross-origin requirement for the embedded same-origin board.

Expose only command kinds authorized by the current operation state and actor: pause, resume, retry failed/blocked work, acknowledge an action-required event, and record a Rule-2-complete deferral; validate release/track/slice identity and closed reason fields before enqueue.

Submit commands only through S04's C-02 service. A duplicate idempotency key returns the original operation; a stale/missing If-Match returns a typed precondition failure; neither path mutates status, Git, events, or notifications twice.

Record public-safe C-03 audit events for authentication session lifecycle, accepted commands, outcomes, and authenticated authorization/precondition rejections with actor, operation ID, target, prior/new revision where applicable, and safe reason classification.

Add accessible mobile controls only when the API advertises an allowed action, with explicit labels, confirmation for deferral, visible pending/success/conflict state, focus restoration, and no optimistic state mutation before the authoritative event/snapshot arrives.

Prove two-process contention, browser preflight/session/CSRF behavior, idempotent retry, stale revision conflict, dark pause reachability, audit completeness, and secret-safe logs with the built binary and real command/event/state services.
out_of_scope: Automatic final integration merge and standing merge delegation are owned by S12 and C-07; this slice exposes no merge command.
A hosted identity provider, OAuth federation, multi-user roles, password recovery, fleet administration, public internet hosting, and TLS certificate issuance are outside #109.
Notification action callbacks are forbidden; S08 mobile notifications open the authenticated board and do not submit C-02 directly.
The S09 read model and S10 read-only board remain authoritative; this slice layers authenticated controls without changing their source semantics.
The twelve planned slices in 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates retain resume-worktree, dry-run, max-turn, design-authority, provider, lint, and guard-fidelity ownership and are not reimplemented here.

effort_complexity.rationale: This is a security-critical public boundary spanning bind/TLS policy, credential storage, sessions, CSRF/origin/CORS, idempotency, optimistic concurrency, command authorization, audit, mobile interaction, and cross-process tests. Both blast radius and emergent risk are high.

risks.description: A remote bind exposes read or mutation routes over cleartext or without effective authentication.
risks.mitigation: Validate non-loopback TLS, secret reference, and exact HTTPS origin before listen; mutation-test removal of each prerequisite and inspect the actual listening address.

risks.description: CSRF, permissive CORS, or a notification link can submit a command using an operator session.
risks.mitigation: Use same-origin by default, SameSite session cookies, exact Origin, session-bound CSRF, explicit credentialed preflight headers with no wildcard, and view-only notification links.

risks.description: A mobile retry or concurrent process applies a command twice or overwrites a newer verifier result.
risks.mitigation: Require durable idempotency key and If-Match revision at the C-02 service; prove duplicate and stale requests under two server/loop processes.

risks.description: The web handler bypasses the command service and directly edits status or spawns a CLI, recreating adapter-specific application logic.
risks.mitigation: Construct handlers with the C-02 interface only, add architecture rules forbidding state/exec imports in web adapters, and verify a fake command service observes every gesture.

risks.description: Authentication material or sensitive command reasons leak through URLs, browser storage, audit, or server logs.
risks.mitigation: Use body/header credentials only, HttpOnly sessions, in-memory CSRF, safe reason allowlists, query/log canaries, and no request-body logging.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHILE remote security configuration is absent or invalid THE SYSTEM SHALL default to loopback and SHALL reject a non-loopback bind before listen unless TLS, an authentication secret reference, and one exact allowed HTTPS origin all validate (C-06, C-08, N-04).

WHEN a valid bootstrap credential is exchanged THE SYSTEM SHALL issue the bounded C-12 session cookie and C-13 session-bound CSRF token without placing either secret in a URL, response log, localStorage, or sessionStorage (C-06, C-08, C-12, C-13, N-04).

IF a state-changing request lacks valid authentication, exact Origin, C-13 CSRF, C-15 Idempotency-Key, application/json, or C-14 If-Match THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it before C-02 enqueue and preserve status revision, Git, events, and notifications (C-02, C-03, C-04, C-06, C-08, C-12, C-13, C-14, C-15, N-02, N-04).

WHEN the same authenticated C-02 request is retried with the same C-15 Idempotency-Key THE SYSTEM SHALL return the original operation ID with at most one state transition, Git effect, event sequence, and notification enqueue (C-01, C-02, C-03, C-04, C-06, C-15, N-02).

IF C-14 If-Match does not equal the current C-04 revision THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed precondition conflict containing the current safe revision and perform no mutation (C-02, C-04, C-06, C-14, N-02, N-04).

WHEN an authenticated operator pauses an eligible active loop from the mobile board THE SYSTEM SHALL submit one C-02 pause command, persist a C-03 audit/lifecycle event, and render the authoritative paused C-04 revision without direct handler file writes or subprocess dispatch (C-02, C-03, C-04, C-06, N-02, N-04; #68).

WHEN two authenticated processes submit different valid commands against the same C-04 revision THE SYSTEM SHALL accept at most one and SHALL return a typed conflict for the other with one linear C-03 audit order (C-02, C-03, C-04, C-06, N-02; #5).

WHEN an authenticated command is accepted, completed, or rejected by authorization/precondition THE SYSTEM SHALL append a public-safe C-03 audit event carrying actor, operation ID when assigned, target, classification, and applicable prior/current revisions (C-02, C-03, C-06, N-02, N-04).

IF authentication, CSRF, command, or audit fixtures contain token, cookie, credential, request-body, model-payload, or absolute-path canaries THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL expose none of them in URLs, API bodies, events, notifications, browser storage, or logs (C-03, C-06, N-04).

WHEN the built binary serves the board on a configured non-loopback TLS fixture THE SYSTEM SHALL complete browser login, exact-origin preflight, one revision-checked pause, one idempotent retry, one stale-revision conflict, and one audit read at a 360-pixel viewport (C-02, C-03, C-04, C-06, C-08, N-02, N-04).
